original_hypothesis.title: GaugeV2.setInternalBribe Tautological Validation Allows Setting address(0) Breaking Fee Distribution
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: setInternalBribe uses `require(_int >= address(0), 'ZA')` which always passes because all addresses are >= address(0) when compared as uint160. This allows setting internal_bribe to address(0). When claimFees is subsequently called, it attempts IERC20.safeApprove(address(0), ...) and IBribe(address(0)).notifyRewardAmount(...), both of which will revert, permanently breaking LP fee distribution for the gauge.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The >= comparison on addresses is a logic error. The developer likely intended `require(_int != address(0))`. The tautological check means any address value is accepted, including zero address.

proof_of_signal.pattern: require(_int >= address(0), 'ZA') -- always true; address comparison >= address(0) is tautological for all addresses

preconditions: Gauge isForPair is true (LP fee claiming enabled)

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: Tautological address comparison always passes zero address check
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: internal_bribe = address(0) causes _claimFees to revert on approve/notifyRewardAmount calls

analysis_questions: Can GaugeFactory.setInternalBribe also trigger this via batch admin call?
Does _claimFees revert or silently fail with address(0)?
What is the recovery path once internal_bribe is set to address(0)?
validation_requirements: Set internal_bribe to address(0) and call claimFees()
Verify revert on safeApprove/notifyRewardAmount to address(0)

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: Owner accidentally or maliciously sets internal_bribe to address(0). All subsequent claimFees calls revert, LP fees accumulate in the pair but cannot be forwarded to bribes.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: LP fees stuck and not distributed to bribe system; gauge fee claim permanently broken
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: All voters who would receive internal bribes from this gauge
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Recoverable by owner calling setInternalBribe with correct address

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No test for setInternalBribe with address(0)

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
